questions.text: Which benefit of cloud computing lets you provision resources in minutes instead of waiting weeks for hardware?
questions.explanation: Agility is the ability to provision and release resources quickly to experiment and respond to change.

questions.text: A company wants to pay only for the compute capacity it uses, with no long-term contracts. Which cloud model supports this?
questions.explanation: Pay-as-you-go converts capital expense to variable operational expense.

questions.text: Which AWS Well-Architected pillar focuses on protecting information and systems?
questions.explanation: The Security pillar covers confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data and systems.

questions.text: What is an Availability Zone?

options.text: A geographic area with multiple isolated locations
One or more discrete data centers with redundant power and networking

questions.explanation: An AZ is one or more data centers in a Region, designed for isolation from failures in other AZs.

questions.text: Which deployment model gives a company exclusive use of cloud infrastructure on-premises while using AWS-managed services patterns?
questions.explanation: Private/hybrid options include VPC-only workloads and AWS Outposts for on-premises AWS infrastructure.

questions.text: Which are advantages of cloud computing? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Trade capital expense for variable expense

questions.explanation: Common advantages include variable expense, elasticity, and global reach; cloud does not eliminate ops or guarantee zero downtime.

questions.text: Which service provides a central view to manage billing, support, and IAM across AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations helps consolidate billing and apply policies across multiple accounts.

questions.text: The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) is primarily used to help organizations with:

options.text: Structured cloud transformation across business capabilities

questions.explanation: CAF provides guidance across perspectives such as business, people, governance, platform, security, and operations.

questions.text: Which statement best describes elasticity in AWS?

options.text: Resources are fixed at purchase time
Resources scale up or down to match demand
Data is encrypted at rest only

questions.explanation: Elasticity is the ability to acquire resources as needed and release them when no longer needed.

questions.text: Edge locations are used primarily by which service to cache content closer to users?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN) that uses edge locations.

questions.text: Which pillar of the Well-Architected Framework focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value?
questions.explanation: Operational Excellence covers operations in production and improvement of procedures.

questions.text: A startup needs to run a web application with minimal server management. Which compute option is most appropriate at a high level?
questions.explanation: Serverless (Lambda) and managed services reduce undifferentiated heavy lifting for new workloads.

questions.text: Which AWS service helps you create and manage AWS accounts with governance guardrails?
questions.explanation: Control Tower automates setup of a multi-account environment with best-practice blueprints.

questions.text: High availability in AWS is best achieved by:

options.text: Deploying in a single Availability Zone

questions.explanation: Multi-AZ designs protect against data center-level failures within a Region.

questions.text: Which tool provides a customized view of AWS service health and alerts for your account?
questions.explanation: Personal Health Dashboard alerts you to events that may affect your resources.

questions.text: The AWS Acceptable Use Policy describes:
questions.explanation: Acceptable Use Policy defines restrictions on use of AWS services.

questions.text: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which task is the customer's responsibility?
questions.explanation: Customers are responsible for security IN the cloud, including guest OS and firewall configuration.

questions.text: Which IAM feature allows temporary security credentials for users and roles?
questions.explanation: STS issues temporary credentials used by roles, federation, and MFA sessions.

questions.text: What does the principle of least privilege recommend?

options.text: Grant administrators all permissions by default
Grant only permissions required to perform a task
Share root account keys across teams

questions.explanation: Least privilege limits access to the minimum needed, reducing blast radius.

questions.text: Which service provides centralized governance and compliance reports for AWS agreements and certifications?
questions.explanation: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports.

questions.text: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) for the AWS account root user is recommended because:

options.text: It adds a layer of protection for the most privileged account
It replaces the need for IAM

questions.explanation: Root user has full account access; MFA helps protect against credential compromise.

questions.text: Which are AWS services or features that help detect security threats? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: GuardDuty finds malicious activity; Security Hub aggregates security findings.

questions.text: AWS KMS is primarily used for:
questions.explanation: AWS Key Management Service manages cryptographic keys for AWS and your applications.

questions.text: Which network feature acts as a virtual firewall at the instance level in a VPC?
questions.explanation: Security groups are stateful firewalls for EC2 instances and ENIs.

questions.text: AWS CloudTrail is used to:

options.text: Log API activity across your AWS account

questions.explanation: CloudTrail records AWS API calls for auditing and governance.

questions.text: Which service helps you analyze, investigate, and identify the root cause of security findings?
questions.explanation: Amazon Detective visualizes security data to investigate potential issues.

questions.text: Encryption of data at rest on Amazon S3 is often implemented using:

options.text: S3 server-side encryption with KMS or S3-managed keys

questions.explanation: S3 supports SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, and client-side encryption options.

questions.text: An IAM policy is:
questions.explanation: Policies are JSON documents that define what actions are allowed or denied.

questions.text: AWS Shield Standard provides:

options.text: DDoS protection for all AWS customers at no additional charge

questions.explanation: Shield Standard automatically protects against common network and transport layer DDoS.

questions.text: Which compliance concept means AWS undergoes third-party audits for standards such as ISO and SOC?
questions.explanation: AWS maintains many compliance certifications customers can inherit via shared responsibility.

questions.text: Federation with corporate identity providers commonly uses:
questions.explanation: IAM Identity Center enables SSO and federation with external identity sources.

questions.text: Network ACLs in a VPC are:

options.text: Stateless filters at the subnet level

questions.explanation: NACLs provide optional stateless allow/deny rules for subnets.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides DDoS protection and web application firewall capabilities?
questions.explanation: AWS WAF filters HTTP/S requests; Shield provides DDoS mitigation.

questions.text: Which service provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) provides virtual servers.

questions.text: Amazon S3 is best described as:
questions.explanation: S3 stores objects in buckets with high durability and scalability.

questions.text: Which database service is fully managed and serverless with automatic scaling?
questions.explanation: DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL database with serverless capacity modes.

questions.text: Amazon RDS is used for:
questions.explanation: RDS manages databases such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server.

questions.text: Which service decouples application components using message queues?
questions.explanation: Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) enables asynchronous messaging.

questions.text: AWS Lambda runs code:

options.text: Without provisioning servers, in response to events
Only inside VPC by default with no config

questions.explanation: Lambda is serverless compute that executes code on demand.

questions.text: Amazon VPC allows you to:

options.text: Provision an isolated network in AWS

questions.explanation: VPC lets you define private subnets, routing, and connectivity.

questions.text: Which service is a managed Kubernetes control plane?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) runs Kubernetes on AWS.

questions.text: Amazon CloudWatch is primarily used for:
questions.explanation: CloudWatch collects metrics, logs, and alarms for AWS resources.

questions.text: Which storage service provides block volumes for EC2 instances?

options.text: Amazon Glacier Instant Retrieval only as block

questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Block Store attaches persistent block storage to EC2.

questions.text: Which are AWS compute services? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: EC2 and Fargate (serverless containers) are compute options; Redshift is analytics.

questions.text: Amazon Route 53 provides:
questions.explanation: Route 53 is a scalable DNS web service.

questions.text: AWS CloudFormation helps you:

options.text: Model and provision infrastructure as code

questions.explanation: CloudFormation templates describe AWS resources for repeatable deployments.

questions.text: Amazon API Gateway is used to:
questions.explanation: API Gateway handles API traffic for REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs.

questions.text: Which service is best for breaking a monolith into microservices with event-driven communication?

options.text: Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive only

questions.explanation: EventBridge is a serverless event bus for connecting application components.

questions.text: Amazon Aurora is:

options.text: A MySQL and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database

questions.explanation: Aurora is a managed relational database with high performance and availability.

questions.text: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is designed to:

options.text: Deploy and scale web applications with minimal infrastructure decisions

questions.explanation: Elastic Beanstalk handles capacity provisioning, load balancing, and scaling for apps.

questions.text: Which pricing model offers the largest discount for steady-state workloads with a 1- or 3-year commitment?
questions.explanation: Reserved capacity and Savings Plans reduce cost for predictable usage.

questions.text: AWS Cost Explorer allows you to:

options.text: Visualize and analyze AWS spending over time

questions.explanation: Cost Explorer provides charts and forecasts for cost and usage.

questions.text: Which support plan includes 24/7 access to Cloud Support Engineers via phone and chat?
questions.explanation: Business and Enterprise plans include 24/7 technical support by phone and chat.

questions.text: The AWS Free Tier:

options.text: Offers limited free usage for new and eligible services under stated terms
Makes all AWS usage free forever

questions.explanation: Free Tier has time and usage limits; always check current terms per service.

questions.text: Consolidated billing in AWS Organizations:

options.text: Combines usage across accounts for volume pricing benefits

questions.explanation: Consolidated billing aggregates charges and can share Reserved Instance benefits.

questions.text: AWS Budgets can:

options.text: Set custom cost and usage thresholds with alerts

questions.explanation: Budgets helps track spend against plans you define.

questions.text: Spot Instances are appropriate when:

options.text: Workloads can tolerate interruption and want lowest price
You need guaranteed capacity for 3 years

questions.explanation: Spot uses spare capacity at a discount; instances can be reclaimed with notice.

questions.text: Which document provides estimates of service pricing for planning?
questions.explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator (formerly Simple Monthly Calculator) estimates costs.

questions.text: AWS Trusted Advisor provides:

options.text: Best-practice recommendations across cost, performance, security, and fault tolerance

questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor inspects your environment and suggests improvements.

questions.text: Which is TRUE about AWS Support Basic plan?

options.text: It is included for all AWS customers at no additional charge
It includes 15-minute response for production down

questions.explanation: Basic support is free and includes customer service and documentation access.

questions.text: AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR) delivers:

options.text: Detailed line-item billing data to an S3 bucket you own

questions.explanation: CUR provides comprehensive billing data for analysis and allocation.

questions.text: Moving data OUT of AWS to the internet generally:

options.text: May incur data transfer out charges
Costs more than data transfer in

questions.explanation: Data transfer pricing varies by service and direction; outbound to internet is often charged.

questions.text: Amazon SNS is used for:
questions.explanation: Simple Notification Service publishes messages to subscribers and other services.

questions.text: AWS Config is used to:

options.text: Assess, audit, and evaluate configurations of AWS resources
Run SQL queries on S3 only

questions.explanation: AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes over time.

questions.text: A Region in AWS is:

options.text: A separate geographic area containing multiple Availability Zones
A single rack in one building only

questions.explanation: Regions are independent geographic areas for deployment and compliance.

questions.text: Amazon Athena allows you to:

options.text: Query data in S3 using standard SQL

questions.explanation: Athena is serverless interactive query service for S3 data.

questions.text: Savings Plans apply to:

options.text: Compute usage in exchange for a commitment to a consistent amount ($/hour)

questions.explanation: Savings Plans offer lower prices on compute in return for a one- or three-year commitment.

questions.text: Which Well-Architected pillar focuses on minimizing the environmental impact of running cloud workloads?
questions.explanation: Sustainability focuses on energy efficiency and reducing waste in cloud architectures.

questions.text: Fault tolerance in the AWS Cloud is most closely associated with which concept?

options.text: Designing for component failure without service interruption

questions.explanation: Fault-tolerant designs continue operating when components fail, often using redundancy across AZs.

questions.text: Which are characteristics of the AWS Cloud? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Fixed physical server ownership by customers

questions.explanation: NIST-style characteristics include on-demand self-service, broad network access, resource pooling, rapid elasticity, and measured service.

questions.text: AWS Service Catalog allows organizations to:

options.text: Create and manage approved catalogs of IT services for users

questions.explanation: Service Catalog helps govern which products users can deploy from approved templates.

questions.text: Which statement describes the AWS shared responsibility model?

options.text: Security and compliance are shared between AWS and the customer
AWS manages guest operating systems on EC2

questions.explanation: AWS secures the cloud; customers secure what they run in the cloud.

questions.text: A company needs to run workloads with the lowest possible operational overhead for an unpredictable web app. Which approach aligns with cloud value?

options.text: Buy data center hardware in advance

questions.explanation: Managed/serverless services reduce undifferentiated heavy lifting and scale with demand.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a single sign-on experience to multiple AWS accounts and business applications?
questions.explanation: IAM Identity Center (AWS SSO) centralizes access to accounts and SAML applications.

questions.text: Disaster recovery strategies on AWS often use:
questions.explanation: Cross-Region replication and backups support RTO/RPO goals for DR.

questions.text: Which benefit describes trading upfront data center costs for pay-as-you-go pricing?

options.text: Variable expense instead of capital expense

questions.explanation: Cloud converts CapEx to OpEx with consumption-based pricing.

questions.text: The AWS Well-Architected Tool is used to:
questions.explanation: The tool helps document and improve architectures using the Well-Architected Framework.

questions.text: Which is an example of horizontal scaling?

options.text: Adding more EC2 instances behind a load balancer
Upgrading to a larger instance size only

questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling adds instances; vertical scaling increases size of one instance.

questions.text: AWS Local Zones are designed to:

options.text: Place compute closer to end users in metro areas

questions.explanation: Local Zones extend AWS infrastructure closer to populations for low-latency workloads.

questions.text: Which pillar emphasizes using computing resources efficiently and adapting to changing requirements?
questions.explanation: Performance Efficiency focuses on efficient use of IT and computing resources.

questions.text: Hybrid cloud typically means:

options.text: Combining on-premises infrastructure with AWS Cloud

questions.explanation: Hybrid connects existing data centers to AWS via VPN, Direct Connect, or Outposts.

questions.text: Which AWS program helps partners deliver solutions to customers?
questions.explanation: APN includes consulting and technology partners that build on AWS.

questions.text: AWS Wickr is categorized under which type of service at a high level?
questions.explanation: Wickr provides encrypted messaging for enterprises (communications/collaboration category).

questions.text: Which describes economies of scale on AWS?

options.text: AWS aggregates usage to pass savings to customers
Each customer builds their own data centers

questions.explanation: Large-scale infrastructure allows AWS to offer competitive pay-as-you-go pricing.

questions.text: Which are pillars of the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability.

questions.text: Amazon Inspector is primarily used to:
questions.explanation: Inspector assesses EC2 and container workloads for vulnerabilities.

questions.text: Amazon Macie helps discover and protect:
questions.explanation: Macie uses machine learning to find sensitive data such as PII in S3.

questions.text: AWS Secrets Manager is designed to:
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager stores credentials and can automate rotation.

questions.text: Which IAM entity is intended for applications and AWS services to call AWS APIs?

options.text: Public access keys in code repos

questions.explanation: Roles provide temporary credentials without embedding long-term keys.

questions.text: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) provides:
questions.explanation: ACM provisions and manages public and private SSL/TLS certificates.

questions.text: Penetration testing of certain AWS services without prior approval is:

options.text: Allowed for a defined list of services under AWS policy

questions.explanation: AWS has a policy describing permitted security testing on specific services.

questions.text: Which are customer responsibilities under the shared responsibility model? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Encrypting customer data in S3 as needed

questions.explanation: Customers manage data, identity, platform configuration, and application security in the cloud.

questions.text: AWS Audit Manager helps:

options.text: Continuously audit AWS usage against controls

questions.explanation: Audit Manager automates evidence collection for audits and compliance frameworks.

questions.text: IAM Access Analyzer helps identify:
questions.explanation: Access Analyzer finds unintended access from outside your account or organization.

questions.text: Which service aggregates security alerts from multiple AWS accounts?
questions.explanation: Security Hub collects findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and partner tools.

questions.text: Encryption of data in transit to S3 is commonly achieved using:
questions.explanation: Use TLS when accessing S3 via REST APIs or the console.

questions.text: The AWS root user should be used:

options.text: Only for tasks that require root credentials

questions.explanation: Create IAM users/roles for daily work; secure root with MFA and minimal use.

questions.text: Amazon Cognito provides:

options.text: User sign-up, sign-in, and access control for web and mobile apps

questions.explanation: Cognito handles user directories, federation, and OAuth/OIDC flows for apps.

questions.text: AWS Firewall Manager is used to:

options.text: Centrally configure WAF rules across accounts

questions.explanation: Firewall Manager applies AWS WAF and VPC security policies across an organization.

questions.text: Which protects against common DDoS attacks on CloudFront and Route 53 at no extra charge?
questions.explanation: Shield Standard is automatically included for all customers.

questions.text: AWS CloudHSM provides:
questions.explanation: CloudHSM offers dedicated HSM instances for keys you manage.

questions.text: Compliance reports for HIPAA, PCI, and similar frameworks are often accessed through:
questions.explanation: Artifact provides compliance documentation customers need for audits.

questions.text: A security group rule change is an example of:
questions.explanation: Customers configure security groups and NACLs for their VPC resources.

questions.text: AWS IAM policy evaluation logic includes:
questions.explanation: By default requests are denied; explicit allow grants access unless explicit deny exists.

questions.text: Which services support encryption? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Amazon VPC Flow Logs only unencrypted

questions.explanation: S3 and EBS support encryption at rest; many services integrate with KMS.

questions.text: AWS Network Firewall is a:
questions.explanation: Network Firewall provides stateful inspection for VPC traffic.

questions.text: Amazon GuardDuty uses primarily:

options.text: Threat detection based on logs and activity

questions.explanation: GuardDuty analyzes CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs for threats.

questions.text: Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) provides:

options.text: Scalable NFS file storage for EC2

questions.explanation: EFS is managed NFS for Linux workloads across AZs.

questions.text: AWS Snowball is used to:

options.text: Transfer large amounts of data into and out of AWS

questions.explanation: Snowball devices provide petabyte-scale offline data migration.

questions.text: Amazon Redshift is a:
questions.explanation: Redshift is a managed analytics warehouse for SQL queries at scale.

questions.text: AWS Glue is primarily a:

options.text: Serverless data integration and ETL service

questions.explanation: Glue discovers, prepares, and integrates data for analytics.

questions.text: Amazon Kinesis is used for:

options.text: Real-time streaming data ingestion and processing

questions.explanation: Kinesis collects, processes, and analyzes streaming data.

questions.text: AWS Step Functions orchestrates:
questions.explanation: Step Functions coordinates Lambda, ECS, and other services in visual workflows.

questions.text: Amazon ElastiCache is used for:
questions.explanation: ElastiCache improves application performance with microsecond latency caching.

questions.text: AWS App Runner deploys:
questions.explanation: App Runner builds and runs container apps from source or image with minimal ops.

questions.text: Amazon Lightsail is best for:

options.text: Simple applications, websites, and dev environments at a fixed price

questions.explanation: Lightsail bundles compute, storage, and networking for easy projects.

questions.text: AWS Systems Manager helps you:

options.text: Manage and operate EC2 and hybrid nodes at scale

questions.explanation: Systems Manager provides patch, run command, parameter store, and inventory features.

questions.text: Amazon EMR is used for:

options.text: Big data processing with Apache Spark and Hadoop

questions.explanation: EMR runs big data frameworks on managed clusters.

questions.text: AWS Fargate allows you to run containers:
questions.explanation: Fargate is serverless compute for ECS and EKS workloads.

questions.text: Amazon WorkSpaces provides:
questions.explanation: WorkSpaces delivers cloud-based Windows or Linux desktops.

questions.text: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) helps:

options.text: Migrate databases to AWS with minimal downtime

questions.explanation: DMS supports homogeneous and heterogeneous database migrations.

questions.text: Amazon MQ is a managed service for:
questions.explanation: Amazon MQ hosts message brokers for apps moving from on-premises messaging.

questions.text: AWS Amplify helps developers:

options.text: Build and deploy full-stack web and mobile apps

questions.explanation: Amplify provides frontend hosting, APIs, auth, and CI/CD for apps.

questions.text: Amazon SageMaker is used for:

options.text: Building, training, and deploying machine learning models

questions.explanation: SageMaker is a broad ML platform for the full model lifecycle.

questions.text: AWS Transit Gateway connects:

options.text: VPCs and on-premises networks through a central hub

questions.explanation: Transit Gateway simplifies network architecture at scale.

questions.text: Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility) is a:
questions.explanation: DocumentDB stores JSON-like documents with MongoDB API compatibility.

questions.text: Which are AWS storage services? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: S3 is object storage; EBS is block storage for EC2.

questions.text: AWS Direct Connect provides:

options.text: Dedicated private connectivity from on-premises to AWS

questions.explanation: Direct Connect establishes private network links to AWS.

questions.text: Amazon CloudFront integrates with which origin at a high level?

options.text: S3, ALB, EC2, and custom origins

questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content from many AWS and custom HTTP origins.

questions.text: AWS Batch runs:

options.text: Batch computing jobs at any scale

questions.explanation: Batch dynamically provisions compute for batch workloads.

questions.text: Amazon OpenSearch Service is used for:
questions.explanation: OpenSearch Service runs OpenSearch/Elasticsearch for search and observability.

questions.text: AWS IoT Core enables:

options.text: Connected devices to interact with cloud applications

questions.explanation: IoT Core is a managed cloud service for billions of devices.

questions.text: Amazon FSx provides:

options.text: Managed third-party file systems (Windows, Lustre, NetApp, OpenZFS)

questions.explanation: FSx offers fully managed Windows File Server, Lustre, ONTAP, and more.

questions.text: AWS CodeDeploy automates:

options.text: Application deployments to EC2, Lambda, and on-premises

questions.explanation: CodeDeploy rolls out application revisions with minimal downtime.

questions.text: Amazon Rekognition provides:

options.text: Image and video analysis using machine learning

questions.explanation: Rekognition detects objects, faces, text, and inappropriate content in media.

questions.text: AWS Site-to-Site VPN connects:

options.text: Your data center to a VPC over an encrypted tunnel

questions.explanation: Site-to-Site VPN creates IPsec tunnels between your network and AWS.

questions.text: Amazon Comprehend is a:
questions.explanation: Comprehend finds insights and relationships in text using ML.

questions.text: The AWS Pricing Calculator helps you:

options.text: Estimate monthly AWS costs before deployment

questions.explanation: Build estimates for services and configurations at calculator.aws.

questions.text: Which pricing model lets you use spare EC2 capacity at up to 90% discount with possible interruption?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances use unused capacity at steep discounts.

questions.text: AWS Developer Support plan is differentiated from Basic by:

options.text: Business hours email access to Cloud Support for technical issues

questions.explanation: Developer adds technical support during business hours via email.

questions.text: Billing alerts based on actual or forecasted spend can be configured in:
questions.explanation: Budgets sends alerts when cost or usage exceeds thresholds you define.

questions.text: Reserved Instance benefits in Organizations consolidated billing:

options.text: Can apply across accounts in the same organization

questions.explanation: RI and Savings Plan discounts can share across linked accounts.

questions.text: AWS Enterprise Support includes:

options.text: Concierge support team and a Technical Account Manager (TAM) option

questions.explanation: Enterprise provides highest level of proactive and reactive support.

questions.text: Which tool gives free recommendations to reduce cost and improve security?
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor checks cost optimization, performance, security, and fault tolerance.

questions.text: Data transfer IN to AWS from the internet is generally:
questions.explanation: Inbound data transfer is often free; outbound charges commonly apply (verify per service).

questions.text: Which are AWS cost management tools? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Cost Explorer and Budgets help analyze and control spending.

questions.text: On-Demand Instances are ideal when:

options.text: You need short-term, flexible compute without upfront payment

questions.explanation: On-Demand has no commitment; pay per second/hour depending on service.

questions.text: AWS Marketplace allows customers to:

options.text: Find, buy, and deploy third-party software on AWS

questions.explanation: Marketplace offers SaaS, AMIs, and data products from independent vendors.

questions.text: Which cloud service model provides the highest level of flexibility and is most parallel with traditional on-premises IT?
questions.explanation: IaaS provides building blocks for cloud IT including networking, computers, and data storage space. It has the highest level of flexibility and is the easiest to parallel with traditional on-premises IT.

questions.text: Which cloud service model removes the need for your organization to manage the underlying infrastructure so you can focus on the deployment and management of your applications?
questions.explanation: PaaS (Platform as a Service) removes the need for your organization to manage the underlying infrastructure so you can focus on the deployment and management of your applications. Examples include AWS Elastic Beanstalk.

questions.text: Which cloud service model describes a completed product that is run and managed by the service provider?
questions.explanation: SaaS (Software as a Service) is a completed product that is run and managed by the service provider. End users do not need to think about how the service is maintained or how the underlying infrastructure is managed.

questions.text: The AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) groups its capabilities into how many perspectives?
questions.explanation: AWS CAF groups its capabilities in six perspectives: Business, People, Governance, Platform, Security, and Operations. The first three are business capabilities; the last three are technical capabilities.

questions.text: Which AWS CAF perspective serves as a bridge between technology and business, focusing on culture, organizational structure, leadership, and workforce to help organizations evolve to a culture of continuous growth?
questions.explanation: The People perspective serves as a bridge between technology and business, accelerating the cloud journey by helping organizations evolve to a culture of continuous growth, learning, and change as business-as-normal. It focuses on culture, organizational structure, leadership, and workforce.

questions.text: Which AWS CAF transformation phase involves identifying transformation opportunities and creating a foundation for your digital transformation?
questions.explanation: Envision is the first CAF transformation phase — it demonstrates how the Cloud will accelerate business outcomes by identifying transformation opportunities and creating a foundation for your digital transformation.

questions.text: In the AWS CAF, which transformation domain focuses on reimagining your operating model by organizing teams around products and value streams and leveraging agile methods?
questions.explanation: The Organization transformation domain involves reimagining your operating model, organizing teams around products and value streams, and leveraging agile methods to rapidly iterate and evolve.

questions.text: Which of the following are Well-Architected Framework general guiding principles? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Avoid automation to keep manual control

questions.explanation: The Well-Architected Framework guiding principles include: stop guessing your capacity needs, test systems at production scale, automate to make architectural experimentation easier, allow for evolutionary architectures, drive architectures using data, and improve through game days.

questions.text: A company wants to move its servers from a VMware Software-defined Data Center (SSDC) to VMware Cloud on AWS without changing the underlying operating model. Which of the 7R migration strategies does this represent?
questions.explanation: Relocate involves moving apps from on-premises to their cloud version, including moving EC2 instances to a different VPC, AWS account, or Region, or transferring servers from VMware SSDC to VMware Cloud on AWS. No cloud optimizations are made.

questions.text: A company migrates an application to AWS by moving it as-is to EC2 instances without any cloud optimizations. Which of the 7R migration strategies does this represent?
questions.explanation: Rehost (also called 'lift and shift') is a simple migration by re-hosting on AWS with no cloud optimizations. The application is migrated as-is. This can save as much as 30% on cost and can be accomplished with AWS Application Migration Service.

questions.text: A company migrates its on-premises database to Amazon RDS without changing the core application architecture, saving time by moving to a fully managed service. Which cloud migration strategy does this represent?
questions.explanation: Replatform ('lift and reshape') involves not changing the core architecture but leveraging some cloud optimizations, such as migrating a database to RDS or an application to Elastic Beanstalk, to save time and money by moving to fully managed services.

questions.text: Which migration strategy involves reimagining how an application is architected using cloud-native features, such as moving from a monolithic application to microservices or serverless architectures?
questions.explanation: Refactor / Re-architect involves reimagining how the application is architected using cloud-native features. It is driven by the need to add features, improve scalability, performance, security, and agility — such as moving from a monolith to microservices or adopting serverless architectures.

questions.text: A company decides to replace its on-premises CRM system with Salesforce.com when migrating to the cloud. Which of the 7R migration strategies does this represent?
questions.explanation: Repurchase ('drop and shop') involves moving to a different product while migrating to the cloud, often to a SaaS platform. It may be expensive in the short term but is quick to deploy. Examples include CRM to Salesforce.com, HR to Workday, or CMS to Drupal.

questions.text: Which cloud migration strategy involves turning off applications that are no longer needed, potentially saving 10-20% of migration costs and reducing the security attack surface?
questions.explanation: Retire involves turning off applications you no longer need, potentially as a result of re-architecting. This reduces the attack surface for security, saves cost (up to 10-20%), and lets you focus on resources that must be maintained.

questions.text: Which AWS service streams desktop applications to users through a web browser without requiring them to connect to a full virtual desktop?
questions.explanation: Amazon AppStream 2.0 is a Desktop Application Streaming Service that delivers applications to any computer through a web browser, without acquiring or provisioning infrastructure. The application is delivered from within a web browser.

questions.text: What is the key difference between Amazon WorkSpaces and Amazon AppStream 2.0?

options.text: WorkSpaces streams individual apps to a browser; AppStream 2.0 provides a full managed desktop
AppStream 2.0 streams individual desktop applications to a web browser; WorkSpaces provides a fully managed virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI)
WorkSpaces is serverless; AppStream 2.0 requires direct EC2 management
AppStream 2.0 is only for mobile devices; WorkSpaces is only for Windows

questions.explanation: AppStream 2.0 streams individual desktop applications to web browsers (no need to connect to a VDI) and works with any device with a browser. WorkSpaces provides a fully managed VDI where users connect to open native or WAM applications, available on-demand or always-on.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a fully managed, centralized solution to automate backups across AWS services, supporting Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR), cross-region backup, and cross-account backup via AWS Organizations?
questions.explanation: AWS Backup is a fully managed service to centrally manage and automate backups across AWS services. It supports on-demand and scheduled backups, PITR, retention periods, lifecycle management, backup policies, cross-region backup, and cross-account backup using AWS Organizations.

questions.text: A disaster recovery strategy that maintains a minimal version of an environment always running in AWS, with only the core functions of the app active and ready to scale, is called:
questions.explanation: Pilot Light keeps only the core functions of the app running at minimal scale in AWS, ready to scale up when needed for failover. It is more expensive than Backup and Restore but has a faster recovery time.

docs.title: Disaster Recovery Options in the Cloud

questions.text: Which disaster recovery strategy provides the fastest recovery time but is also the most expensive, running a full version of the application at full size in both on-premises and AWS simultaneously?
questions.explanation: Multi-Site / Hot-Site runs a full version of the application at full size in both environments simultaneously. It provides the fastest recovery time (near-zero downtime) but is the most expensive strategy.

docs.title: Disaster Recovery Options in the Cloud

questions.text: Which disaster recovery strategy runs a full version of the application in AWS but at minimum size, ready to scale up to handle production load during a failover?
questions.explanation: Warm Standby runs a full version of the application in AWS at minimum size. During a disaster, the environment is scaled up to handle the full production load. It offers a balance between cost and recovery speed.

docs.title: Disaster Recovery Options in the Cloud

questions.text: Which AWS service provides continuous block-level replication to quickly recover physical, virtual, and cloud-based servers into AWS, protecting against ransomware attacks and disasters?
questions.explanation: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (formerly CloudEndure Disaster Recovery) provides continuous block-level replication for your servers, quickly and easily recovering physical, virtual, and cloud-based servers into AWS. It protects critical databases and enterprise apps against ransomware.

questions.text: Which AWS service is used to move large amounts of data from on-premises storage to AWS (Amazon S3, EFS, and FSx), with replication tasks that can be scheduled hourly, daily, or weekly?
questions.explanation: AWS DataSync moves large amounts of data from on-premises to AWS. It can synchronize to Amazon S3 (any storage class including Glacier), Amazon EFS, and Amazon FSx for Windows. Replication tasks can be scheduled hourly, daily, or weekly, and are incremental after the first full load.

questions.text: Which AWS service helps plan migration projects by gathering server utilization data and dependency mapping information from on-premises data centers using either agentless or agent-based discovery?
questions.explanation: AWS Application Discovery Service helps plan migration projects by gathering information about on-premises data centers. It supports Agentless Discovery (VM inventory, configuration, performance history) and Agent-based Discovery (system configuration, running processes, network connections). Data can be viewed within AWS Migration Hub.

questions.text: Which AWS service is the evolution of AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) and provides a lift-and-shift rehost solution that converts physical, virtual, and cloud-based servers to run natively on AWS with minimal downtime?
questions.explanation: AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) is the AWS evolution of CloudEndure Migration, replacing AWS Server Migration Service (SMS). It provides a lift-and-shift (rehost) solution that converts physical, virtual, and cloud-based servers to run natively on AWS with minimal downtime and reduced costs.

questions.text: Which AWS service helps build a data-driven business case for migration to AWS by providing a clear baseline of your organization's current on-premises running state through an agentless collector?
questions.explanation: AWS Migration Evaluator helps you build a data-driven business case for migration to AWS. It installs an Agentless Collector to take a snapshot of on-premises footprint, gathers inventory and resource utilization data, and provides Quick Insights reports for customized cost insights and guidance for a business case.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a central location to collect server and application inventory data, assess and plan migrations, track migration status from multiple migration tools, and orchestrate migrations using pre-built templates?
questions.explanation: AWS Migration Hub is a central location to collect server and application inventory data for assessment, planning, and tracking of migrations to AWS. AWS Migration Hub Orchestrator provides pre-built templates for enterprise apps like SAP and SQL Server, and supports status updates from Application Migration Service and DMS.

questions.text: Which AWS service lets you run controlled fault injection experiments based on Chaos Engineering principles, using pre-built templates to create disruptive events like sudden CPU spikes, helping you uncover hidden bugs and performance bottlenecks?
questions.explanation: AWS Fault Injection Simulator (FIS) is a fully managed service for running fault injection experiments on AWS workloads. Based on Chaos Engineering, it helps uncover hidden bugs and performance bottlenecks by stressing applications with disruptive events and observing system responses. It supports EC2, ECS, EKS, and RDS.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a fully managed global network of satellite ground stations, allowing you to control satellite communications and download satellite data directly to your AWS VPC within seconds?
questions.explanation: AWS Ground Station is a fully managed service that lets you control satellite communications, process data, and scale satellite operations. It provides a global network of satellite ground stations near AWS regions, allowing you to download satellite data to your AWS VPC within seconds and send it to S3 or EC2.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a scalable 2-way marketing communications platform supporting email, SMS, push, voice, and in-app messaging, with the ability to create message templates, delivery schedules, and full campaigns targeting customer segments?
questions.explanation: Amazon Pinpoint is a scalable 2-way (outbound/inbound) marketing communications service supporting email, SMS, push, voice, and in-app messaging. Unlike SNS/SES where you manage each message's audience and content individually, Pinpoint lets you create message templates, delivery schedules, targeted segments, and full campaigns.

questions.text: Which AWS service uses GraphQL to store and sync data across mobile and web apps in real-time, supports offline data synchronization, and integrates with DynamoDB and Lambda?
questions.explanation: AWS AppSync stores and syncs data across mobile and web apps in real-time using GraphQL. It provides real-time subscriptions, offline data synchronization (replacing Cognito Sync), fine-grained security, and integrates with DynamoDB and Lambda. AWS Amplify can leverage AppSync in the background.

questions.text: Which AWS service allows you to visually design and build serverless applications and generates Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using CloudFormation, without needing to be an AWS expert?
questions.explanation: AWS Infrastructure Composer lets you visually design and build serverless applications quickly on AWS without needing to be an AWS expert. It generates Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using CloudFormation and can import existing CloudFormation/SAM templates to visualize them.

questions.text: Which AWS service allows you to test web and mobile applications against real desktop browsers and mobile devices concurrently — not emulators — with the ability to configure device settings like GPS, language, and Wi-Fi?
questions.explanation: AWS Device Farm is a fully managed service that tests web and mobile apps against real desktop browsers, real mobile devices, and tablets (not emulators). Tests can run concurrently on multiple devices, and you can configure device settings such as GPS, language, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth.

questions.text: Which AWS service replaced the original AWS Forums and provides a crowd-sourced, expert-reviewed Q&A platform for AWS technical questions, where community members earn reputation points?
questions.explanation: AWS re:Post is an AWS-managed Q&A service offering crowd-sourced, expert-reviewed answers to technical questions about AWS, replacing the original AWS Forums. Community members earn reputation points by providing accepted answers. Questions from Premium Support customers without community responses are escalated to AWS Support engineers.

questions.text: AWS Right Sizing is best described as:

options.text: Automatically scaling EC2 instances up and down based on demand
The process of matching instance types and sizes to workload performance and capacity requirements at the lowest possible cost
Purchasing Reserved Instances for all workloads to maximize savings
Migrating on-premises servers to AWS to reduce infrastructure costs

questions.explanation: AWS Right Sizing is the process of matching instance types and sizes to your workload performance and capacity requirements at the lowest possible cost. It includes looking at deployed instances and identifying opportunities to eliminate or downsize without compromising capacity. Tools like CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, and Trusted Advisor can help.

questions.text: Which AWS tool allows customers to track, measure, review, and forecast the carbon emissions generated from their AWS usage to help meet sustainability goals?
questions.explanation: The AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool tracks, measures, reviews, and forecasts carbon emissions generated from your AWS usage. It provides visibility into carbon emissions by geography and by service over time, helping you meet your own sustainability goals on your path to 100% renewable energy.

questions.text: Which AWS service allows customers to quickly find and engage AWS Certified third-party experts for on-demand project work, with billing integrated directly into their AWS bill?
questions.explanation: AWS IQ lets customers quickly find professional help from AWS Certified third-party experts for on-demand project work. It provides video-conferencing, contract management, secure collaboration, and integrated billing — charges are added directly to your AWS bill per milestone.

questions.text: Which AWS offering provides a team of AWS experts that fully manages infrastructure and application support on AWS, handling routine tasks such as monitoring, patch management, security, and backups around the clock?
questions.explanation: AWS Managed Services (AMS) provides infrastructure and application support on AWS with a team of AWS experts who manage and operate your infrastructure for security, reliability, and availability 24/365. AMS handles change requests, monitoring, patch management, security, and backup services, reducing operational overhead and risk.

questions.text: Which AWS service converts text into lifelike speech, enabling you to create applications that talk?
questions.explanation: Amazon Polly converts text to lifelike speech (text-to-audio). It is the reverse of Transcribe, which converts speech to text.

questions.text: Which AWS service automatically converts speech or audio into text, commonly used for generating subtitles?
questions.explanation: Amazon Transcribe is an automatic speech recognition (ASR) service that converts audio to text. Common use cases include generating subtitles and transcribing customer calls.

questions.text: Which AWS service is used to build conversational chatbots and voice interfaces powered by the same technology as Amazon Alexa?
questions.explanation: Amazon Lex provides automatic speech recognition and natural language understanding to build conversational chatbots and voice interfaces. It powers Amazon Alexa and can integrate with Amazon Connect for contact center bots.

questions.text: Which AWS ML-powered service provides an intelligent search engine that can search across unstructured text, FAQs, and documents to return relevant answers?
questions.explanation: Amazon Kendra is an ML-powered enterprise search service that returns highly accurate answers from unstructured data such as documents, FAQs, and manuals — going beyond keyword search to understand natural language queries.

questions.text: Which AWS service delivers real-time personalized product and content recommendations using the same machine learning technology used by Amazon.com?
questions.explanation: Amazon Personalize enables developers to build applications with the same real-time personalized recommendation technology used by Amazon.com, without requiring ML expertise.

questions.text: Which AWS service uses machine learning to automatically extract text, handwriting, and structured data (such as forms and tables) from scanned documents?
questions.explanation: Amazon Textract goes beyond simple OCR to detect and extract text and data from documents, including forms and tables. It can process PDFs and scanned images to extract structured data automatically.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides automatic language translation, enabling you to localize content for international users?
questions.explanation: Amazon Translate is a neural machine translation service that provides fast, high-quality, and affordable language translation, enabling you to localize content for international users.

questions.text: Which of the following are AWS machine learning services? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Amazon Polly (text-to-speech) and Amazon Textract (document text extraction) are both ML services. VPC, CloudFormation, and Route 53 are networking/infrastructure services.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides serverless business intelligence (BI) dashboards, visualizations, and ad hoc analysis with built-in ML insights?
questions.explanation: Amazon QuickSight is a cloud-native, serverless BI service that lets you create and publish interactive dashboards and visualizations. It scales to thousands of users and includes ML-powered insights such as anomaly detection and forecasting.

questions.text: Which AWS managed database service is purpose-built for storing and navigating highly connected data, such as social networks, fraud detection graphs, and recommendation engines?
questions.explanation: Amazon Neptune is a fully managed graph database service optimized for storing and querying highly connected datasets. Common use cases include social networking, fraud detection, knowledge graphs, and recommendation engines.

questions.text: Which AWS managed database service is purpose-built for storing and analyzing time-series data such as IoT sensor readings, DevOps metrics, and application telemetry?
questions.explanation: Amazon Timestream is a fast, scalable, and serverless time-series database service for IoT and operational applications. It is optimized for storing and analyzing data that changes over time, such as sensor readings, DevOps metrics, and application event logs.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides an in-memory cache that sits in front of DynamoDB to deliver microsecond response times for read-heavy workloads?
questions.explanation: Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) is a fully managed, highly available in-memory cache specifically for DynamoDB that delivers up to 10x performance improvement, reducing response times from milliseconds to microseconds — even at millions of requests per second.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a fully managed blockchain network supporting the Hyperledger Fabric and Ethereum frameworks?
questions.explanation: Amazon Managed Blockchain is a fully managed service that allows you to create and manage scalable blockchain networks using the popular open-source frameworks Hyperledger Fabric and Ethereum.

questions.text: Which Amazon S3 storage class is designed for data that is accessed less frequently but requires rapid access when needed, offering lower storage cost than S3 Standard with a retrieval fee?
questions.explanation: S3 Standard-IA (Infrequent Access) is for data accessed less frequently but that requires rapid access when needed. It has a lower per-GB storage price than S3 Standard but charges a per-GB retrieval fee. Ideal for backups, disaster recovery files, and long-lived data.

questions.text: Which Amazon S3 storage class automatically moves objects between access tiers based on changing access patterns, with no retrieval fees and no operational overhead?
questions.explanation: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between multiple access tiers based on actual access patterns — from Frequent Access to Infrequent Access to Archive tiers — with no retrieval fees and no minimum storage duration for the Frequent/Infrequent tiers.

questions.text: A company needs the lowest-cost Amazon S3 storage for long-term archival data that is rarely accessed and can tolerate retrieval times of 12 hours or more. Which storage class should they use?
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost S3 storage class, designed for long-term data archiving that is accessed once or twice a year. Retrieval times range from 12 to 48 hours. It is ideal for compliance archives and digital preservation.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid Amazon S3 storage classes? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Valid S3 storage classes include: S3 Standard, S3 Intelligent-Tiering, S3 Standard-IA, S3 One Zone-IA, S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval, S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval, and S3 Glacier Deep Archive. Provisioned IOPS and Cold HDD are EBS volume types, not S3 classes.

questions.text: Which AWS service is a fully managed private Docker container image registry for storing, managing, and deploying container images?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Container Registry (ECR) is a fully managed Docker container registry that makes it easy to store, manage, share, and deploy container images. It integrates with ECS, EKS, and Fargate.

questions.text: What is the primary difference between Amazon ECS and Amazon EKS?

options.text: ECS uses Docker; EKS does not support containers
ECS is AWS's proprietary container orchestration service; EKS runs managed Kubernetes
EKS is only for serverless workloads; ECS requires EC2
ECS is for Windows only; EKS is for Linux only

questions.explanation: Amazon ECS (Elastic Container Service) is AWS's own container orchestration service. Amazon EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service) is a managed Kubernetes service. Both can run containers on EC2 or Fargate. Use ECS for AWS-native simplicity and EKS when you need Kubernetes compatibility.

questions.text: Which AWS service is a fully managed source control service that hosts Git-based repositories?
questions.explanation: AWS CodeCommit is a fully managed source control service that hosts secure Git-based repositories. It integrates with other AWS developer tools and eliminates the need to manage your own source control system.

questions.text: Which AWS service is a fully managed continuous integration service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces software packages?
questions.explanation: AWS CodeBuild is a fully managed continuous integration (CI) service that compiles source code, runs tests, and produces deployment-ready software packages. You pay only for the build minutes you use.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides a fully managed continuous delivery pipeline that orchestrates build, test, and deploy phases every time there is a code change?
questions.explanation: AWS CodePipeline is a fully managed continuous delivery service that automates the build, test, and deploy phases of your release process every time there is a code change. It integrates with CodeCommit, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, and third-party tools.

questions.text: Which AWS service is a fully managed artifact repository that allows teams to securely store, publish, and share software packages and dependencies used in their software development process?
questions.explanation: AWS CodeArtifact is a fully managed software artifact repository service that allows organizations to securely store and share packages used in their software development process. It works with common package managers like npm, pip, Maven, and NuGet.

questions.text: Which AWS service helps developers analyze and debug distributed applications by tracing requests as they travel through your application, identifying performance bottlenecks?
questions.explanation: AWS X-Ray provides distributed tracing for applications, helping developers analyze and debug production, distributed applications such as those built using a microservices architecture. It creates a service map showing request flows and identifies performance bottlenecks.

questions.text: Which AWS CloudTrail feature uses ML to automatically detect unusual API activity patterns in your AWS account and alert you to potentially unauthorized actions?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail Insights automatically detects unusual operational activity in your AWS account by analyzing CloudTrail write management events. It uses ML models to detect anomalies such as spikes in resource provisioning or IAM actions.

questions.text: Which AWS service provides automated code reviews for identifying critical issues like security vulnerabilities and application performance recommendations using ML?
questions.explanation: Amazon CodeGuru is an ML service for automated code reviews and application performance recommendations. CodeGuru Reviewer identifies critical issues and bugs in code, while CodeGuru Profiler identifies the most expensive lines of code in your application.

questions.text: Which AWS service improves global application availability and performance by routing user traffic through the AWS global network using static anycast IP addresses?
questions.explanation: AWS Global Accelerator uses the AWS global network to optimize the path from users to your applications, providing two static anycast IP addresses that route traffic to the optimal AWS endpoint. Unlike CloudFront (which caches content), Global Accelerator improves performance for non-cacheable content and TCP/UDP applications.

questions.text: Which S3 feature accelerates uploads and downloads to Amazon S3 by routing traffic through CloudFront edge locations and the AWS backbone network?
questions.explanation: S3 Transfer Acceleration enables fast, easy, and secure transfers of files over long distances between your client and an S3 bucket. It routes data through CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations, then sends it to S3 over optimized AWS network paths.

questions.text: Which AWS infrastructure deployment option brings AWS compute, storage, and database services to the edge of a 5G network for ultra-low latency applications?
questions.explanation: AWS Wavelength brings AWS compute and storage services to the edge of 5G networks, enabling developers to build applications that deliver single-digit millisecond latencies to mobile devices and end users over the 5G network.

questions.text: Which AWS service extends AWS infrastructure, services, APIs, and tools to customer on-premises data centers, allowing you to run AWS services locally?
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts brings native AWS services, infrastructure, and operating models to virtually any data center, co-location space, or on-premises facility. AWS installs and manages the Outposts rack in your facility, letting you run AWS workloads on-premises.

questions.text: Which AWS services are used to extend AWS infrastructure beyond a standard Region? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: AWS Outposts extends AWS to on-premises data centers. AWS Local Zones place compute, storage, and other services closer to end users in metro areas. Both extend the AWS footprint beyond a standard Region, unlike RDS, SQS, and IAM which are standard regional/global services.

questions.text: Which EC2 purchasing option provides a physical server fully dedicated to your use, helping meet compliance requirements that do not support multi-tenant virtualization?
questions.explanation: An EC2 Dedicated Host is a physical server with EC2 instance capacity fully dedicated to your use. Unlike Dedicated Instances (which run on hardware dedicated to you but don't give visibility into the host), Dedicated Hosts give you visibility and control over the physical server — useful for BYOL licensing and compliance requirements.

questions.text: Which AWS cost management service uses machine learning to continuously monitor your spending patterns and alert you when it detects anomalous charges, helping you catch unexpected costs early?
questions.explanation: AWS Cost Anomaly Detection uses ML to continuously monitor your cost and usage to detect unusual spends. It can send alerts when anomalies are detected, helping you catch unexpected costs from misconfigurations or unauthorized usage before they become large bills.

questions.text: Which AWS service analyzes your EC2, Lambda, EBS, and ECS resource configurations and utilization metrics to recommend optimal resource types that reduce costs while maintaining performance?
questions.explanation: AWS Compute Optimizer uses ML to analyze historical utilization data and recommends optimal AWS resource configurations for EC2 instances, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, Lambda functions, and ECS services on Fargate — helping reduce costs while improving performance.

questions.text: Which AWS service allows you to view and manage your default service quotas (formerly called limits) and request quota increases, and can alert you when you are close to a limit?
questions.explanation: AWS Service Quotas is a central location where you can view and manage your quotas (limits) for AWS services. You can request quota increases and create CloudWatch alarms to notify you when you are approaching a quota threshold.

questions.text: Which AWS VPC feature allows you to create a private connection between your VPC and another AWS service (like S3 or DynamoDB) without requiring traffic to traverse the public internet?
questions.explanation: AWS PrivateLink (accessed via VPC Endpoints) provides private connectivity between VPCs and AWS services without requiring traffic to traverse the public internet. Interface endpoints use PrivateLink for AWS services; Gateway endpoints are used for S3 and DynamoDB.

questions.text: What is the key difference between AWS CloudTrail and Amazon CloudWatch?

options.text: CloudTrail monitors performance metrics; CloudWatch records API calls
CloudTrail records API calls and account activity for auditing; CloudWatch monitors performance metrics and logs for operational visibility
CloudWatch is only for billing; CloudTrail is only for security

questions.explanation: CloudTrail records who did what — API calls, user actions, and account activity — primarily for governance, compliance, and auditing. CloudWatch monitors how resources are performing — metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards — primarily for operational visibility and troubleshooting.

questions.text: An AWS customer has a legal requirement to host data only within a specific country. Which Well-Architected consideration does this represent?

options.text: Performance Efficiency — choosing regions closer to users
Data residency and compliance — choosing an AWS Region that meets regulatory requirements
Sustainability — minimizing carbon footprint by region

questions.explanation: AWS Regions are geographically isolated. When choosing a Region, data residency and compliance requirements — such as GDPR in Europe — often dictate which Region to use. Data does not leave a Region unless you explicitly choose to move it.

questions.text: Which AWS IAM feature generates a report that lists all IAM users in your account and the status of their credentials, including passwords, access keys, and MFA devices?
questions.explanation: An IAM Credential Report is a downloadable CSV report that lists all IAM users in your account and the status of their various credentials — including whether passwords and access keys are enabled, when they were last used, and whether MFA is enabled. It helps with auditing and compliance.

questions.text: Which AWS design principle recommends breaking a large application into smaller, independent components so that a failure in one part does not cascade to other parts?
questions.explanation: Loose coupling is an AWS cloud design principle where components are designed to be independent. A change or failure in one component should not cascade to other components. Services like SQS (queues) and SNS (notifications) help achieve loose coupling between application layers.
